message:
Last winter, a big storm rolled in faster than the forecast said. My family group chat *exploded* — my mom asking if we had flashlights, my brother sending photos of his flooded gutters, my sister asking "wait, do we still have that battery-powered radio?" It was kind of chaotic and also kind of perfect, because it meant we actually *talked* about what we had and what we didn't.

Turned out we were missing a few basics, and honestly? Having that conversation before the power went out (which it did, for like 8 hours) was way less stressful than scrambling in the dark.

I'm not saying you need to be military-level prepared — just that a little gentle check-in with your household about the essentials (water, light, first aid, meds, battery stuff) makes a real difference. No drama needed.

Does your family have a go-to person who usually handles this stuff, or is it more of a team effort at your place?
